<summary>
  <request>Retry calendar event creation after authorization error with Google Calendar integration</request>
  <investigated>Google Calendar integration authorization scope issue - the integration was originally connected with read-only permissions, preventing event creation or modification. Root cause identified as insufficient OAuth scopes granted during initial authorization on claude.ai.</investigated>
  <learned>Claude.ai Google Calendar integrations require explicit "modify events" permission during OAuth authorization. Simply re-authorizing from the Google account settings is insufficient - the integration must be disconnected and reconnected through claude.ai Settings → Integrations to properly grant write permissions. Alternative workaround exists using .ics file export/import for bulk calendar operations (16 events mentioned).</learned>
  <completed>Diagnosed persistent authorization error as structural OAuth scope limitation. Provided two solutions: (1) disconnect/reconnect workflow through claude.ai integration settings, (2) .ics file generation for manual import into Google Calendar.</completed>
  <next_steps>Awaiting user decision on whether to generate .ics file for immediate workaround or proceed with reconnecting the integration for proper write access.</next_steps>
  <notes>Session is in Italian. The recurring error suggests multiple retry attempts before this checkpoint. The .ics option provides immediate relief for the 16 pending appointments while the integration reconnection would solve the underlying permission issue permanently.</notes>
</summary>
